question: Name the planets in our solar system in order from the Sun.

answers.display: Mercury — 36 million miles from the Sun (1st)
Venus — 67 million miles from the Sun (2nd)
Earth — 93 million miles from the Sun (3rd)
Mars — 142 million miles from the Sun (4th)
Jupiter — 484 million miles from the Sun (5th)

question: Name the most abundant elements in the universe by mass.

answers.display: Hydrogen — ~75% of all normal matter in the universe
Helium — ~24% of all normal matter in the universe
Oxygen — ~1% of all normal matter in the universe (3rd most abundant)
Carbon — 4th most abundant element in the universe
Neon — 5th most abundant element in the universe

question: Name the most abundant elements in Earth's crust by mass.

answers.display: Oxygen — 46% of Earth's crust by mass
Silicon — 28% of Earth's crust by mass
Aluminum — 8% of Earth's crust by mass
Iron — 5% of Earth's crust by mass
Calcium — 4% of Earth's crust by mass

question: Name the largest moons in our solar system by diameter.

answers.display: Ganymede (Jupiter) — 3,273 miles diameter (largest moon, bigger than Mercury)
Titan (Saturn) — 3,200 miles diameter
Callisto (Jupiter) — 2,995 miles diameter
Io (Jupiter) — 2,264 miles diameter
Earth's Moon — 2,159 miles diameter

question: Name the brightest stars visible from Earth.

answers.display: Sirius — magnitude −1.46 (brightest star in the night sky)
Canopus — magnitude −0.74 (2nd brightest)
Rigil Kentaurus (Alpha Centauri) — magnitude −0.27 (3rd brightest)
Arcturus — magnitude −0.05 (4th brightest)
Vega — magnitude 0.03 (5th brightest)

question: Name the most abundant gases in Earth's atmosphere by volume.

answers.display: Nitrogen — 78% of Earth's atmosphere
Oxygen — 21% of Earth's atmosphere
Argon — 0.93% of Earth's atmosphere
Carbon dioxide — 0.04% of Earth's atmosphere
Neon — 0.0018% of Earth's atmosphere (5th most abundant)

question: Name the longest bones in the human body.

answers.display: Femur (thigh bone) — avg. 19 inches (longest bone in the body)
Tibia (shin bone) — avg. 16 inches
Fibula (outer lower leg) — avg. 15 inches
Humerus (upper arm) — avg. 14 inches
Radius (forearm) — avg. 10 inches

question: Name the largest organs in the human body by weight.

answers.display: Skin — avg. 8–10 lbs (largest organ by weight and surface area)
Liver — avg. 3.3 lbs
Brain — avg. 3.0 lbs
Lungs — avg. 2.5 lbs (both combined)
Heart — avg. 0.7 lbs

question: Name the most common blood types in the United States by prevalence.

answers.display: O positive — 37.4% of the US population (most common)
A positive — 35.7% of the US population
B positive — 8.5% of the US population
AB positive — 3.4% of the US population
O negative — 6.6% of the US population (universal donor)

question: Name the elements with the highest melting points.

answers.display: Tungsten — 6,192°F melting point (highest of any element)
Rhenium — 5,756°F melting point
Osmium — 5,491°F melting point
Tantalum — 5,463°F melting point
Molybdenum — 4,753°F melting point

question: Name the fastest animals on Earth by top speed.

answers.display: Cheetah — 70–75 mph (fastest land animal)
Pronghorn — 55 mph (fastest land animal in the Western Hemisphere)
Springbok — 55 mph
Wildebeest — 50 mph
Lion — 50 mph

question: Name the largest land animals by weight.

answers.display: African Bush Elephant — up to 13,000 lbs (largest land animal)
Hippopotamus — up to 9,900 lbs
White Rhinoceros — up to 5,100 lbs
Giraffe — up to 3,000 lbs
Gaur (Indian Bison) — up to 3,300 lbs (largest bovine)

question: Name the largest animals in the ocean by weight.

answers.display: Blue Whale — up to 330,000 lbs (largest animal ever known to exist)
Fin Whale — up to 150,000 lbs
Sperm Whale — up to 125,000 lbs
Right Whale — up to 100,000 lbs
Whale Shark — up to 47,000 lbs (largest fish)

question: Name the tallest animals in the world.

answers.display: Giraffe — up to 19 ft tall (tallest living animal)
African Elephant — up to 13 ft at the shoulder
Ostrich — up to 9 ft tall (tallest bird)
Brown Bear — up to 9 ft tall when standing upright
Moose — up to 7.5 ft at the shoulder

question: Name the longest-living animals in the world.

answers.display: Ocean Quahog Clam — 500+ years (oldest confirmed: 507 years)
Greenland Shark — 400+ years (longest-lived vertebrate)
Bowhead Whale — 200+ years
Giant Tortoise — 180+ years (longest-lived land animal)
Rougheye Rockfish — 150+ years

question: Name the fastest birds in the world by top speed.

answers.display: Peregrine Falcon — 240 mph in a dive (fastest animal on Earth)
Golden Eagle — 150–200 mph in a dive
White-throated Needletail — 105 mph in level flight (fastest in level flight)
Eurasian Hobby — 100 mph
Frigatebird — 95 mph

question: Name the largest primates in the world by weight.

answers.display: Eastern Gorilla — up to 440 lbs (largest living primate)
Western Gorilla — up to 400 lbs
Orangutan — up to 285 lbs
Common Chimpanzee — up to 130 lbs
Gelada (bleeding-heart monkey) — up to 44 lbs (largest Old World monkey)

question: Name the heaviest flightless birds in the world.

answers.display: Ostrich — up to 345 lbs (heaviest bird alive)
Southern Cassowary — up to 167 lbs
Emu — up to 130 lbs
Emperor Penguin — up to 99 lbs (heaviest penguin)
Greater Rhea — up to 88 lbs

question: Name the most commonly fractured bones in the human body.

answers.display: Clavicle (collarbone) — most frequently broken bone in the body
Radius (forearm) — most common wrist/arm fracture
Ankle bones (fibula/tibia) — most common lower-leg fractures
Hip (femoral neck) — most common fracture in the elderly
Vertebrae — most common fractures from osteoporosis and compression

question: Name the most commonly diagnosed cancers in the United States.

answers.display: Breast cancer — 300,000+ new cases/year (most diagnosed cancer overall)
Prostate cancer — 290,000+ new cases/year
Lung cancer — 235,000+ new cases/year (leading cause of cancer death)
Colorectal cancer — 153,000+ new cases/year
Melanoma (skin cancer) — 100,000+ new cases/year
